sanskrit:
यदा भूतपृथग्भावमेकस्थमनुपश्यति।
तत एव च विस्तारं ब्रह्म सम्पद्यते तदा॥
transliteration: yadā bhūta-pṛthag-bhāvam eka-stham anupaśyati | tata eva ca vistāraṃ brahma sampadyate tadā ||
literal_meaning: When one beholds all beings as abiding in the One and their expansion from That alone, then one attains to Brahman.
contextual_meaning: The mind that stops fragmenting life into separate, competing identities discovers a single field of awareness; from this unified perception the sense of universal selfhood naturally emerges.
psychological_state: Post-fragmentation integration: the observer ceases to project categorical boundaries and experiences the continuum of being.
trigger_scenario: After a heated diversity-training workshop you silently watch commuters on a subway platform and suddenly feel every story, body, and breath as pulses of the same living field.
anchor_text: See every being resting in one awareness and arising from it.
